title: धन्वन्तरि हृदयम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री धन्वन्तरि हृदयम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्री धन्वन्तरिहृदयस्तोत्रस्य अश्विनौ ऋषी।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। धन्वन्तरिर्देवता।

ॐ धन्वन्तरये विद्महे वैद्यराजाय धीमहि।
तन्नो धन्वन्तरिः प्रचोदयात्॥

हृदयं मे सदा पातु धन्वन्तरिरमृतप्रदः।
मम देहं सदा रक्षेत् वैद्यनाथो महेश्वरः॥ १॥

प्राणान् मे रक्षतु सदा क्षीरसागरसम्भवः।
आयुर्मे वर्धयत्वेष विष्णोरंशः सनातनः॥ २॥

सर्वरोगान् हरत्वेष सर्वव्याधिविनाशनः।
सर्वदोषान् शमयतु सर्वधातून् प्रपोषयेत्॥ ३॥

वातपित्तकफान् हन्तु शिरोरोगं विनाशयेत्।
नेत्ररोगं हरत्वेष कर्णरोगं च नाशयेत्॥ ४॥

हृद्रोगं शमयत्वेष उदररोगं विनाशयेत्।
त्वग्रोगान् नाशयत्वेष अस्थिरोगान् विनाशयेत्॥ ५॥

मज्जारोगं हरत्वेष शुक्ररोगं विनाशयेत्।
सर्वाङ्गरोगान् हरतु रक्तरोगं च नाशयेत्॥ ६॥

ज्वरं हरतु मे नित्यं कासं श्वासं च नाशयेत्।
प्रमेहं शूलरोगं च सर्वव्याधिं विनाशयेत्॥ ७॥

इदं हृदयमज्ञात्वा धन्वन्तरिं न पूजयेत्।
यः पठेत् प्रातरुत्थाय सर्वरोगविवर्जितः॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री धन्वन्तरि हृदयम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Dhanvantari Hridayam ||

Om Asya Shri Dhanvantari Hridaya Stotrasya Ashvinau Rishi |
Anushtup Chhandah | Dhanvantarir Devata |

Om Dhanvantaraye Vidmahe Vaidyarajaya Dhimahi |
Tanno Dhanvantarih Prachodayat ||

Hridayam Me Sada Patu Dhanvantarir Amritapradah |
Mama Deham Sada Rakshet Vaidyanatho Maheshvarah || 1 ||

Pranan Me Rakshatu Sada Kshirasagara Sambhavah |
Ayurme Vardhayatvesa Vishnor Amshah Sanatanah || 2 ||

Sarvarogan Haratvesa Sarvavyadhi Vinashanah |
Sarvadoshan Shamayatu Sarvadhatin Praposayet || 3 ||

Vatapittakaphan Hantu Shirorogam Vinashayet |
Netrarogam Haratvesa Karnarogam Cha Nashayet || 4 ||

Hridrogam Shamayatvesa Udararogam Vinashayet |
Tvagrogan Nashayatvesa Asthirogan Vinashayet || 5 ||

Majjarogam Haratvesa Shukrarogam Vinashayet |
Sarvanga Rogan Haratu Raktarogam Cha Nashayet || 6 ||

Jvaram Haratu Me Nityam Kasam Shvasam Cha Nashayet |
Prameham Shularogam Cha Sarvavyadhim Vinashayet || 7 ||

Idam Hridayam Ajnatva Dhanvantarim Na Pujayet |
Yah Pathet Pratarutthaya Sarvaroga Vivarjitah || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Dhanvantari Hridayam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
मेरे हृदय की सदा रक्षा करें अमृतप्रद धन्वन्तरि। वैद्यनाथ महेश्वर मेरे देह की सदा रक्षा करें।

क्षीरसागर से उत्पन्न सदा मेरे प्राणों की रक्षा करें। विष्णु के सनातन अंश मेरी आयु बढ़ाएँ।

सर्वरोगनाशक सब रोग हरें, सब दोष शमन करें, सब धातुओं को पुष्ट करें।

वात-पित्त-कफ को हनें, शिरोरोग, नेत्ररोग, कर्णरोग को नाश करें।

हृद्रोग, उदररोग, त्वग्रोग, अस्थिरोग, मज्जारोग, शुक्ररोग, रक्तरोग — सब नाश करें।

ज्वर, कास (खाँसी), श्वास, प्रमेह, शूल — सब व्याधि नाश करें।

जो प्रातःकाल इस हृदय स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है, वह सर्वरोगमुक्त होता है।
benefits: Heart-essence (hridayam) stotra of Lord Dhanvantari for complete healing
Systematically invokes healing for every body system and organ
Addresses tridosha, saptadhatu, specific diseases by name
Invokes freedom from all diseases and perfect health
Ideal for patients, health practitioners and Ayurveda devotees
description: Dedicated to Lord Dhanvantari, the Dhanvantari Hridayam (धन्वन्तरि हृदयम्) is a heart-essence prayer revered in Hindu tradition. Attributed to Agni Purana / Ayurvedic Tradition, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to heart-essence (hridayam) stotra of Lord Dhanvantari for complete healing and address tridosha, saptadhatu, specific diseases by name. It is also said to grant freedom from all diseases and perfect health. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Dhanteras and Dhanvantari Jayanti. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Dhanvantari Hridayam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dhanvantari Hridayam and why is it called a heart-essence hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanvantari Hridayam (धन्वन्तरि हृदयम्) is an eight-verse hridaya-stotra — the 'heart-essence' hymn — of Lord Dhanvantari, the divine physician of the gods. The text is preserved in the classical Agni Purana / Ayurvedic samhita tradition. Its viniyoga declares: the Ashvini Kumaras (the twin physicians of the devas, themselves disciples of Dhanvantari) are the rishis, Anushtup is the chhanda, and Sri Dhanvantari is the devata. The hymn begins with the celebrated Dhanvantari Gayatri — Om Dhanvantaraye vidmahe vaidyarajaya dhimahi tanno Dhanvantarih prachodayat — and then methodically invokes Dhanvantari to protect, sustain and heal each organ, each dhatu and each disease-category by name. It is called hridaya because it is the concentrated essence of all Ayurvedic prayer to the Adi-Vaidya. (Sources: Agni Purana; Ayurvedic upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Dhanvantari Hridayam invoke healing for every body system?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hridayam is unique among healing stotras for its systematic, organ-by-organ invocation of healing power. Verse 1 — hridayam me sada patu (may he ever protect my heart); verse 2 — pranan me rakshatu (may he protect my pranas); verse 3 — sarva-doshan shamayatu sarva-dhatun praposayet (may he pacify all three doshas and nourish all seven dhatus); verse 4 — vata-pitta-kaphan hantu, shiroroga vinashayet, netraroga and karnaroga (vata-pitta-kapha imbalance, headache, eye-disease, ear-disease); verse 5 — hridroga, udararoga, tvagroga, asthiroga (heart-disease, abdominal disease, skin-disease, bone-disease); verse 6 — majjaroga, shukraroga, sarvanga-roga, raktaroga (marrow-disease, reproductive-disease, full-body-disease, blood-disease); verse 7 — jvara, kasa, shvasa, prameha, shularoga, sarva-vyadhi (fever, cough, asthma, diabetes, colic, all diseases). Few stotras in Sanatana Dharma offer such complete pathological coverage. (Sources: Dhanvantari Hridayam verses 1-7.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dhanvantari Gayatri included in this hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanvantari Hridayam opens with the celebrated Dhanvantari Gayatri — Om Dhanvantaraye vidmahe vaidyarajaya dhimahi, tanno Dhanvantarih prachodayat. Word-meaning: Dhanvantaraye vidmahe — 'we know Lord Dhanvantari'; vaidyarajaya dhimahi — 'we meditate upon the King of physicians'; tanno Dhanvantarih prachodayat — 'may that Dhanvantari illumine and inspire us.' This Gayatri is one of the deva-Gayatris of the classical 24-Gayatri set and is recited as the principal mantra of the entire hymn. The Hridayam essentially expands this seed-Gayatri into an eight-verse healing kavacha. Patients, doctors, Ayurveda students and pharmacists chant it 11, 21 or 108 times daily, especially before preparing or administering medicines. (Sources: Deva-Gayatri tradition; Ayurveda paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Dhanvantari Hridayam different from the Dhanvantari Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: While both are eight-verse hymns to Lord Dhanvantari, their orientation differs significantly. The Dhanvantari Ashtakam is structured as a stuti — a praise-hymn — that celebrates Dhanvantari's divine attributes (Adi-deva, Kshiroda-mathana-udbhuta, founder of Ashtanga Ayurveda, master of tridosha-vidya, giver of dirgha-ayus). The Dhanvantari Hridayam, by contrast, is structured as a kavacha-hridaya — a healing armour — that systematically invokes the Lord's protective and curative power for each part of the body and each named disease. Where the Ashtakam praises Dhanvantari's identity, the Hridayam petitions his clinical action — naming hridroga, udararoga, jvara, kasa, shvasa, prameha by name. The traditional practice is to recite the Ashtakam first (for darshana of the deity) and then the Hridayam (for the healing application). (Sources: Dhanvantari upasana tradition; comparative stotra study.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Dhanvantari Hridayam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanvantari Hridayam is most powerfully recited on Dhanteras (Dhanvantari Jayanti — Kartik Krishna Trayodashi) — the supreme festival of the deva-vaidya, two days before Diwali; on Tuesdays (Mangalvar — when blood and inflammation are addressed); on every Pradosh; on Sundays for surya-related healing; and continuously by patients suffering chronic illness, by parents of sick children, by families during epidemics, and by Ayurveda doctors before consultation. The verse 8 itself declares — Idam hridayam ajnatva Dhanvantarim na pujayet, yah pathet pratarutthaya sarva-roga-vivarjitah — 'one should not even worship Dhanvantari without knowing this Hridayam; one who recites it on rising in the morning becomes free of all disease.' Recitation should be done after bath, facing east, with a ghee lamp before a Dhanvantari image. (Sources: Dhanvantari Hridayam verse 8; Ayurvedic paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Dhanvantari Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Ayurveda and Smarta acharyas attribute the following benefits to sincere Dhanvantari Hridayam recitation: targeted relief from every named roga in the verses — head, eye, ear, heart, abdomen, skin, bone, marrow, reproductive, full-body and blood diseases; specific relief from jvara (fever), kasa (cough), shvasa (asthma/breathlessness), prameha (diabetes and urinary disorders) and shula (colic, joint pain); pacification of vata-pitta-kapha tridosha imbalance; nourishment and balancing of saptadhatu (rasa, rakta, mamsa, meda, asthi, majja, shukra); enhancement of the action of medicines and treatments being received; akala-mrityu-haram (prevention of untimely death); dirgha-ayus and arogya for the entire family when recited collectively; and the supreme phala — recognition that Dhanvantari is the inner-Vaidya, the Atman as Vishnu, who alone heals the bhava-roga of samsara. (Sources: Dhanvantari Hridayam phalashruti; Ayurveda tradition.)
